content.text: Earth is the third planet from the Sun.
The Moon is a planet.
Water freezes at 0°C at standard pressure.
Humans need oxygen to breathe.
A year has exactly 300 days.
Fish are mammals.

true-false-medium.title: Reading carefully
true-false-medium.instruction: Some statements are long—read before you answer.

content.text: Photosynthesis is the process plants use to make food from sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide.
If a map’s legend is missing, every color and symbol on that map will always mean the same thing in every country without any explanation.
A triangle with three equal sides is called an equilateral triangle.
The water cycle includes evaporation, condensation, and precipitation as major stages students learn in elementary science.
Because magnets can attract some metals, every metal object in a classroom—including pure copper wire and aluminum foil—will stick strongly to a typical bar magnet.
An author writes a book; an illustrator often creates the pictures that go with the story.
Midnight is the middle of the afternoon.
When we reduce, reuse, and recycle, we can help lower waste that ends up in landfills, though recycling rules still differ by community.

true-false-medium.notes: Long statements + one image; layout and timing readability.

true-false-stress.title: Imbalance and repeat-until-time
true-false-stress.instruction: Mostly true on purpose; editor should warn. Player may loop with timer.

content.text: One plus one equals two.
The sun rises in the east.
Books can be borrowed from a library.
Plants need light to grow well.
Ice is colder than boiling water.
A square has four sides.
We use our ears to hear.
Friday comes after Thursday.
A bicycle usually has two wheels.
The single false statement in this stress set: penguins are insects.

Long stress line: when scientists carefully measure rainfall with a rain gauge over many days, they can describe weather patterns for a region, compare seasons, and help communities plan for floods or dry spells without claiming that any single afternoon storm predicts the entire year.

Another long true line: classroom routines such as lining up quietly, listening when others speak, and putting materials away help everyone learn, stay safe, and feel respected during busy school days.

true-false-stress.notes: Imbalance warning (mostly true). High speed + repeat-until-time + short countdown. Long statements at end.
